_comment: sp_Blitz CheckID to maturity category/level mapping. Exported from DiagnosticsRoadmap CategoryLevelMap + sql-checks.json. Edit this file to reclassify findings — do NOT commit if it contains confidential server/org info.

blitzCheckMap.business_translation: Joins sys.databases to msdb.dbo.backupset to find databases with no full backup in the past week. A missing backup means RPO is effectively unbounded , any failure results in total, unrecoverable data loss. Check msdb.dbo.backupset and confirm the backup job is active in SQL Agent.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Full Recovery Model Without Log Backups
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: Detects databases in FULL or BULK_LOGGED recovery that have no transaction log backup in msdb.dbo.backupset. Without log backups the log chain is broken: point-in-time restore is impossible, and the log file grows unboundedly until disk space is exhausted. Schedule log backups every 15â€“60 minutes depending on RPO requirement.

msdb.dbo.backupset , checks whether the backup history table has grown beyond a threshold (typically > 10,000 rows without purge). Large backup history tables slow down backup/restore commands because SQL reads from this table for every operation. Run EXEC msdb.dbo.sp_delete_backuphistory to purge. Set a retention policy via SQL Agent maintenance plan.

sys.server_principals where is_sysadmin = 1. sysadmin is the most privileged SQL Server role , equivalent to root. Accounts with sysadmin have unrestricted access to all databases, all server objects, and can impersonate any login. List via SELECT name, type_desc FROM sys.server_principals WHERE IS_SRVROLEMEMBER('sysadmin',name)=1. Remove sysadmin from any account that does not absolutely require it; use principle of least privilege.

sys.server_role_members where role_principal_id = SUSER_ID('securityadmin'). securityadmin members can grant any permission and create logins , effectively elevating to sysadmin capability. Treat securityadmin membership as equivalent to sysadmin for risk purposes. Review all securityadmin members and reduce to those that genuinely require it.

. msdb.dbo.sysjobs where owner_sid != SYSLOGIN('sa'). Jobs owned by user accounts can fail if the owner's login is disabled or dropped. Additionally, the job runs with the permissions of the owner in certain contexts. Standardise job ownership to SA: EXEC msdb.dbo.sp_update_job @job_name=N'...', @owner_login_name=N'sa'.

sys.objects with startup extended stored procedure (XP) configuration. Startup stored procedures execute automatically at instance start with sysadmin privileges. A malicious actor who can create/modify startup procs gains persistent code execution. Audit: EXEC sp_procoption @ProcName=N'<proc>', @OptionName='startup', @OptionValue='true' , remove any undocumented startup procs.

sys.server_audits WHERE is_state_enabled = 1. SQL Server Audit is configured and running. Informational , auditing is a positive security control. Verify: (1) audit destination (file, application log, or security log), (2) audit action groups cover required events (SCHEMA_OBJECT_ACCESS_GROUP for data access, LOGIN_CHANGE_PASSWORD_GROUP for security events), (3) audit file path has sufficient disk space, (4) ON_FAILURE = CONTINUE vs SHUTDOWN SERVER (critical environments should use SHUTDOWN to prevent unaudited access).

sys.endpoints. Verify each configured endpoint: type_desc (TSQL, DATABASE_MIRRORING, SERVICE_BROKER, HTTP), state_desc, and authentication. Unused endpoints should be dropped. HTTP endpoints in particular (rare since SQL 2012) represent a significant unauthenticated attack surface if misconfigured.

sys.resource_governor_configuration where is_enabled = 1. Resource Governor partitions CPU and memory across workload groups. A misconfigured Resource Governor can silently throttle critical workloads below acceptable thresholds. Review sys.dm_resource_governor_resource_pools and sys.dm_resource_governor_workload_groups for current resource limits and actual usage.

sys.server_triggers where is_disabled = 0. Server-level DDL triggers fire on DDL events across the entire instance (CREATE TABLE, ALTER LOGIN, etc.). They add overhead to every DDL operation. A poorly-written server trigger can cause deadlocks or block administrative operations. Audit each trigger's purpose and performance impact.

sys.databases where is_auto_close_on = 1. AUTO_CLOSE evicts the database from memory when all connections close. On next connection, SQL re-opens the database files, rebuilds the system-table cache, and re-allocates buffer pool pages. This cold-start penalty causes I/O spikes and increased latency on OLTP databases with short connection bursts. Disable: ALTER DATABASE [db] SET AUTO_CLOSE OFF.

sys.databases where is_auto_shrink_on = 1. AUTO_SHRINK periodically attempts to reduce file size, generating massive random read I/O as it moves pages from the end of the file forward. Immediately after shrink, the released space is reallocated on next load, causing the file to grow again. This creates a perpetual shrink/grow cycle that fragments indexes and stresses I/O. Disable: ALTER DATABASE [db] SET AUTO_SHRINK OFF. Never use SHRINKDATABASE except in extreme emergencies.

Reads default trace for DBCC events. Cross-checks whether DBCC CHECKDB was run without the WITH PHYSICAL_ONLY option on very large databases (causing excessive runtime) or with DATA_PURITY skipped. Ensure the maintenance script runs WITH DATA_PURITY on databases migrated from older versions.

sys.databases where is_auto_create_stats_on = 0. Auto-create statistics allows SQL Server to create single-column statistics on columns used in WHERE, JOIN, and ORDER BY clauses. Without it, the optimizer has no cardinality information for unindexed columns and may choose full scans or bad join orders. Disable only if you are manually managing all statistics.

sys.databases where is_auto_update_stats_on = 0. Auto-update statistics triggers a statistics update when 20% of rows change (or dynamic threshold on SQL 2016+). Without it, statistics become stale and the optimizer makes increasingly bad decisions as data changes. Never disable without a documented replacement (scheduled manual updates with Ola Hallengren).

sys.databases where is_auto_update_stats_async_on = 1. Async stats updates allow a query to run with the old (stale) stats while the update runs in the background. The query may get a bad plan this execution, but the next execution will use fresh stats. Good for OLTP where a blocking synchronous stats update would cause query timeouts. Acceptable trade-off , not a problem per se.

sys.databases where is_published = 1 or is_subscribed = 1 or is_distributor = 1. Replication is active. Monitor distribution latency, agent status, and undistributed commands in the distribution database (msdb..MSdistribution_commands). Replication failures accumulate silently until the distribution database fills or latency breaches SLA thresholds.

sys.databases where is_date_correlation_on = 1. Date correlation optimization maintains statistics about date column correlations between tables for a small set of designated tables. Rarely used. Adding overhead to datetime column updates. Confirm it is actively used or disable: ALTER DATABASE [db] SET DATE_CORRELATION_OPTIMIZATION OFF.

sys.databases where is_encrypted = 1. Informational , TDE is enabled. Key point: the TDE certificate must be independently backed up. A lost certificate permanently prevents decryption of the database files.

sys.master_files where physical_name LIKE 'C:%' for system databases (master, model, msdb). System databases on C: (OS drive) create I/O contention with the OS. More critically, if C: fills, SQL Server may be unable to write to system databases, causing instance failures. Move system databases to a dedicated drive.

sys.master_files where physical_name LIKE 'C:%' for tempdb. TempDB on the C: drive (OS drive) shares I/O with the Windows OS, event logs, and page file. Tempdb is typically the highest-I/O database. Move tempdb data and log files to a dedicated high-performance drive. Also: if C: fills up, the OS can crash.

sys.master_files where physical_name LIKE 'C:%' for user databases. User databases on the OS drive create I/O contention and risk the OS drive filling to capacity. Move database files to a dedicated data volume.

sys.objects WHERE database_id = db_id('master') AND type IN ('U','V','P'). User-created tables in master complicate upgrades and instance refreshes. Objects in master persist across database backups, making recovery scenarios more complex. Move user objects to a dedicated administration database.
sys.objects in msdb database , user-created tables beyond the standard msdb schema. User tables in msdb are at risk of being overwritten during SQL Server upgrades. Move to a dedicated DBA database.

sys.objects in model database. Objects in model are copied to every new database created. Unintended objects in model propagate to all future databases, creating unexpected schema and potentially sensitive data in new databases. Audit and clean model regularly.

msdb.dbo.sysalerts , not all recommended sp_Blitz alerts are configured. sp_Blitz recommends a baseline set of SQL Agent alerts covering: severity 17â€“25, error 823 (I/O error), 824 (consistency error), 825 (read retry), 832 (corrupt page). Run sp_Blitz's alert setup script or Brent Ozar's 'sp_Blitz_Alerts' to configure the complete recommended alert set.

msdb.dbo.sysoperators , no enabled operator with an email address. Alerts without operators have nowhere to send notifications. Create an operator: EXEC msdb.dbo.sp_add_operator @name=N'DBA Team', @email_address=N'dba@company.com'. Configure Database Mail first (sp_configure 'Database Mail XPs').

sys.triggers joined to sys.objects. Triggers add hidden overhead to DML operations , they fire within the transaction, increasing lock hold duration. Multiple nested triggers can cause deadlocks. Auditing triggers (logging every INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) are a common source of blocking on high-throughput tables. Review trigger logic for set-based vs cursor-based patterns.

SERVERPROPERTY('EditionID') , detects Enterprise Edition features (Advanced HADR, partitioning, data compression, resource governor, online operations) in use on a non-Enterprise licence. If the server is downgraded to Standard Edition, these features stop working. Audit Enterprise feature usage before any edition downgrade.

sys.dm_exec_cached_plans where usecounts = 1 AND objtype = 'Adhoc'. A high count of single-use plans means the plan cache is full of non-reusable ad hoc SQL, evicting useful plans. Enable sp_configure 'optimize for ad hoc workloads' = 1 (stores plan stubs instead of full plans on first execution). Also investigate application parameterisation , ensure queries use sp_executesql or parameterised queries.

sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats , io_stall_read_ms / num_of_reads > threshold. Reads averaging more than 10ms indicate a storage read latency problem. Causes: overloaded SAN, insufficient read cache, or non-SSD storage for OLAP workloads. Identify affected files: SELECT DB_NAME(database_id), file_id, io_stall_read_ms/num_of_reads FROM sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats(NULL,NULL) WHERE num_of_reads > 0 ORDER BY io_stall_read_ms/num_of_reads DESC.

sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats , io_stall_write_ms / num_of_writes. Writes averaging > 10ms indicate storage subsystem saturation or misconfiguration. Check: (1) SAN/storage controller queue depth, (2) log drive shared with data files, (3) SQL write-ahead logging pattern vs storage write-back cache, (4) backup I/O competing with workload. Use sys.dm_io_pending_io_requests to catch active stalls.

sys.indexes where index_id = 0 (heap). Heaps have no physical row ordering. Updates cause forwarded records (row moves to new page with a pointer at the old location), degrading read performance over time. Range scans require full table scans. Almost every table benefits from a clustered index. Identify top heap tables by row count and access patterns via sys.dm_db_index_usage_stats.

sys.tables where name matches patterns like '%bak%', '%backup%', '%old%', '%copy%', '%temp%'. Shadow tables are often created during schema changes and never cleaned up. They consume storage, confuse developers, and may contain sensitive data subject to retention policies. Confirm with application owners and drop or archive.

STATS_DATE() across sys.stats shows statistics last updated beyond the staleness threshold. Stale statistics lead to incorrect cardinality estimates, causing the query optimiser to choose suboptimal join orders, serial plans where parallel would be better, and incorrect memory grants. Use sp_updatestats or Ola Hallengren's maintenance solution for statistics updates on a schedule.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Multiple Log Files on One Drive
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.master_files grouped by volume_mount_point , multiple log files on the same physical drive. Multiple log files for one database on the same drive give no throughput benefit (log writes are always sequential to a single active VLF). Multiple log files on the same drive just add management complexity. Drop secondary log files if they are empty: DBCC SHRINKFILE([log2], EMPTYFILE).

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Uneven File Growth Settings in One Filegroup
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.master_files grouped by volume_mount_point , data files in the same filegroup on the same drive have unequal autogrow settings. SQL's proportional fill algorithm distributes writes based on free space; unequal file sizes or growth settings cause I/O hotspots on the largest file. Pre-size files equally and set identical fixed-increment autogrow values.

sys.dm_exec_query_stats: queries with join type forced (LOOP JOIN, HASH JOIN, MERGE JOIN hints). Forced join type hints prevent the optimizer from choosing a better join strategy as data volumes change. These are typically workarounds for parameter-sensitive plans or outdated statistics. Document which queries use join hints via sys.dm_exec_query_plan.

sys.dm_exec_query_stats / sys.dm_exec_sql_text: queries with OPTION(FORCE ORDER) in plan XML. JOIN hints force the optimizer to use a specific join order regardless of statistics. A query that needed a join hint typically has bad statistics or a schema design issue. The hint may give good performance for one set of parameters but poor performance for others. Investigate the underlying statistics quality and index coverage.

sys.indexes where is_hypothetical = 1. Hypothetical indexes are leftover artifacts from the Database Engine Tuning Advisor (DTA). They consume space in sys.indexes and sys.stats, add overhead to statistics maintenance, and may confuse query plan analysis tools. Drop them: SELECT 'DROP INDEX ' + name + ' ON ' + OBJECT_NAME(object_id) FROM sys.indexes WHERE is_hypothetical = 1.

sys.dm_exec_query_stats or Query Store top consumers by CPU, logical reads, or duration. These queries are the highest-impact targets for performance tuning. Each should be reviewed for missing indexes (sys.dm_db_missing_index_details), implicit conversions (sys.dm_exec_query_plan), and parameter sensitivity.

sys.foreign_keys where is_not_trusted = 1. Untrusted foreign keys (created WITH NOCHECK or previously disabled) are not enforced for existing rows. The optimizer cannot use FK metadata to eliminate unnecessary join steps. Re-enable: ALTER TABLE [child] WITH CHECK CHECK CONSTRAINT [fk_name]. This validates all existing rows. A failed validation means orphaned rows exist , clean them before re-enabling.

sys.servers where is_linked = 1. Each linked server uses an OLE DB connection and a security context (specific login or pass-through). Linked server queries are not optimised across boundaries , they pull full tables or execute remote procedures. Audit for unused linked servers and remove them.

sp_configure 'max server memory' set to a value that consumes nearly all physical RAM. When SQL takes too much memory, Windows memory pressure triggers the memory notification object, causing SQL to voluntarily release buffer pool pages. This creates a feedback loop of cache thrashing. Formula: max_server_memory = TotalRAM_MB - (OS_reservation_MB + other_services_MB). Minimum OS reservation: 1 GB + (1 GB per 4 GB RAM above 16 GB).

sys.dm_os_sys_info physical_memory_in_use_kb and max_server_memory_mb from sp_configure. If max_server_memory is below a calculated safe minimum (RAM minus OS reservation), SQL may not have enough buffer pool for the working set. Set max server memory = physical_memory_kb * 0.85 (leave ~15% for OS). Monitor via sys.dm_os_memory_clerks and DBCC MEMORYSTATUS.

Reads Windows Server Failover Cluster event log or sys.dm_hadr_availability_group_states. Recent failovers indicate cluster instability , hardware faults, storage path loss, network flapping, or OS-level issues on the primary node. Each failover causes a brief outage. Review Windows System event log and cluster validation report.

sys.servers where is_linked = 1. Linked servers expose the SQL instance to remote data sources via OLE DB. Each linked server connection uses a security context (mapped login or pass-through). Linked server queries bypass query parameterisation, cannot use local statistics, and can cause distributed deadlocks. Audit each linked server: SELECT * FROM sys.servers; verify the data_source, provider, and catalog. Disable unused linked servers.

sys.databases where owner_sid does not map to a known login or maps to an unexpected principal. A database owned by a low-privileged account or a deleted login creates issues with cross-database ownership chaining. Standardise ownership to SA: ALTER AUTHORIZATION ON DATABASE::[db] TO sa.

sys.check_constraints where is_not_trusted = 1. An untrusted CHECK constraint was disabled or created WITH NOCHECK , SQL Server does not guarantee existing rows satisfy the constraint. The optimizer cannot use untrusted constraints for query simplification (constraint elimination). Re-enable: ALTER TABLE [t] WITH CHECK CHECK CONSTRAINT [c]. This validates all existing rows and marks the constraint trusted.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: SQL Agent Job Runs at Startup
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: msdb.dbo.sysjobs where enabled = 1 and start_step_id = 1 with schedule_id matching 'startup'. SQL Agent jobs configured to run at SQL Server startup. These run before any login-based access and with Agent's service account permissions. Review all startup jobs for legitimacy and document their purpose.

msdb.dbo.sysalerts where has_notification = 0 AND message_id IS NOT NULL. An alert is configured but has no notification operator attached , it triggers silently with no action. Add an operator notification to every configured alert: EXEC msdb.dbo.sp_add_notification @alert_name=N'...', @operator_name=N'DBA Team', @notification_method=1.

sys.indexes where fill_factor != 0 AND fill_factor != 80 (or similar threshold). A non-default fill factor affects every index page , too low wastes space and increases I/O; too high (100) causes frequent page splits and fragmentation. Confirm fill factor is intentionally set based on the insert pattern of each table. Document the rationale.

msdb.dbo.sysalerts , no alert configured for severity 19â€“25 errors. Severity 19 and above indicates serious server-level problems (resource exhaustion, access violations, fatal errors). Without alerts, DBAs discover these events in the error log after the fact. Configure alerts for all severity levels 19â€“25.

sys.databases where compatibility_level is more than one version behind the instance. Very old compatibility levels (e.g. 80, 90) prevent access to critical performance features. sp_Blitz flags databases still on SQL 2000 or 2005 compat mode. These databases cannot use parallel DML, filtered indexes, or modern join optimisations.

sys.master_files comparing log file size to data file size. A log file larger than the data file is unusual , it typically indicates: (1) full recovery without log backups (log chain never truncated), (2) a long-running transaction holding VLFs open, or (3) excessive update/delete activity. Investigate log_reuse_wait_desc in sys.databases. Fix: take a log backup, then DBCC LOGINFO to check VLF count; shrink only after the log_reuse_wait clears.

system_health Extended Events session for xml_deadlock_report. Deadlocks are happening daily , a persistent application-level bug or indexing problem. Capture the full deadlock graph XML via sys.fn_xe_file_target_read_file on the system_health target. Identify the victim resource and both blocking chains. Typical fix: add a covering index to eliminate the shared lock, or change the transaction order to be consistent.

sys.databases where state_desc = 'EMERGENCY'. Emergency mode is set manually (ALTER DATABASE SET EMERGENCY) to access a suspect database for forensic reads. A database left in emergency mode is read-only and not accessible to the application. Investigate the original failure, restore from backup, or run DBCC CHECKDB WITH REPAIR_ALLOW_DATA_LOSS.

sys.databases where is_auto_shrink_on = 1. AUTO_SHRINK periodically checks whether more than 25% of the file is free, then moves pages from the tail of the file to reclaim space. This generates massive random read + write I/O proportional to database size, fragments every index in the database, and causes the file to grow again under normal load , creating a perpetual shrink/grow cycle. Disable immediately: ALTER DATABASE [db] SET AUTO_SHRINK OFF. Never use SHRINKFILE as a routine operation.

sys.databases where owner_sid != SYSLOGIN('sa'). Database owners affect cross-database ownership chaining behaviour. A non-SA owner can cause unexpected permission failures when ownership chaining is relied upon. Standardise database ownership to SA: ALTER AUTHORIZATION ON DATABASE::[db] TO sa.

Queries msdb.dbo.suspect_pages and cross-references last DBCC CHECKDB dates from msdb.dbo.backupset or DBCC DBINFO(). A check more than 7 days old means undetected page-level corruption could exist. Schedule DBCC CHECKDB weekly minimum (nightly for critical DBs). Use WITH PHYSICAL_ONLY for large databases to reduce runtime. Monitor via sys.dm_db_mirroring_auto_page_repair for automated page repair activity.

DBCC LOGINFO() , counts VLF (Virtual Log File) entries. Databases with hundreds or thousands of VLFs experience: slow database startup recovery (SQL must process each VLF), slow log backups, and high log I/O overhead. VLF proliferation is caused by excessive log file autogrow events (especially 1 MB growth increments). To fix: take a full backup, shrink the log (DBCC SHRINKFILE on the log), then grow it in one large increment.

sys.service_queues where is_poison_message_handling_on = 1. Poison message handling in Service Broker automatically disables a queue after 5 consecutive transaction rollbacks. If a queue is disabled, message processing stops silently. Monitor sys.service_queues for is_receive_enabled = 0. Fix the failing message handler before re-enabling the queue.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: The partitioned database may have non-aligned indexes
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.partitions or sys.indexes joined to sys.partition_schemes , non-aligned indexes detected on a partitioned table. Non-aligned indexes cannot be maintained per-partition (partition switching is blocked). Rebuilding non-aligned indexes requires the entire table to be locked. Align all indexes to the partition scheme to enable partition-level operations.

sys.databases where collation_name != SERVERPROPERTY('Collation'). A database collation different from the instance collation causes implicit conversion failures when comparing tempdb objects (which inherit server collation) to database objects. This can prevent string comparisons and break dynamic SQL that joins to tempdb. Standardise database collation during the next planned maintenance window.

sys.indexes joined to detect exact duplicate index definitions (same key columns, same include columns, same filters). Duplicate indexes double the write overhead (every INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE must maintain both). SQL Server cannot choose between them and may use the less efficient one. Drop the duplicate after confirming it is not used by specific query hints (sys.dm_db_index_usage_stats).

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Transaction Log Larger than Data File
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.databases where is_trustworthy_on = 1. TRUSTWORTHY enables EXECUTE AS and CLR assemblies within the database to access server-level resources. A db_owner in a TRUSTWORTHY database can escalate to sysadmin via impersonation without an explicit sysadmin grant. Disable on all databases unless a specific feature (e.g. cross-database Service Broker) explicitly requires it. Use WITH EXECUTE AS LOGIN alternatives where possible.

sys.databases where is_implicit_transactions_on = 1. Implicit transactions mode (SET IMPLICIT_TRANSACTIONS ON) causes DML statements to automatically begin a transaction without an explicit BEGIN TRAN. If the application does not issue COMMIT, the transaction remains open indefinitely, holding locks. Disable implicit transactions in the database; fix the application to use explicit transactions.

sys.master_files for filegroup with multiple data files of significantly different sizes. SQL Server round-robins data writes across proportional-fill files. Unequal file sizes mean one file fills before others, creating hotspots. Equalise file sizes by growing smaller files to match, then set all to autogrow by the same fixed increment.

sys.sql_modules where definition LIKE '%WITH RECOMPILE%'. Procedures with WITH RECOMPILE recompile on every execution, adding CPU overhead. Acceptable for procedures with highly variable parameters but inappropriate for stable, high-frequency procedures. Replace WITH RECOMPILE with OPTION(OPTIMIZE FOR UNKNOWN) or OPTION(RECOMPILE) at individual statement level where needed.

sys.databases where is_auto_close_on = 1. AUTO_CLOSE shuts down and flushes the database when all connections close, releasing its memory. On next connection, the database must re-open and reload its buffer pool. Creates I/O spikes and login latency. Disable on all OLTP databases: ALTER DATABASE [db] SET AUTO_CLOSE OFF.

sys.master_files where max_size > 0 (max file size configured). A maximum file size cap silently stops the database from growing when it reaches the limit , causing 'Could not allocate space' errors that immediately impact the application. Remove max size restrictions: ALTER DATABASE [db] MODIFY FILE (NAME='...', MAXSIZE=UNLIMITED).

sys.databases where is_auto_update_stats_async_on = 0 AND is_auto_update_stats_on = 0. Specifically: async statistics update is disabled. When a query triggers a synchronous statistics update, it must wait for the update to complete before executing , adding latency proportional to table size. On large tables, synchronous stats updates can take minutes. Enable async stats update as a compromise between accuracy and latency.

sys.master_files where growth > 0 and is_percent_growth = 1. Percent-based autogrow events double or grow files proportionally , a 1 GB file with 10% growth adds 100 MB, but a 100 GB file adds 10 GB. Large autogrow events cause long I/O stalls (if IFI is not enabled for data files) and VLF fragmentation in log files. Change all files to fixed-size autogrow: ALTER DATABASE [db] MODIFY FILE (NAME='...', FILEGROWTH=256MB).

sys.databases where is_subscribed = 1 (replication subscriber). The database is receiving changes from a replication publisher. Replication adds background load via the distribution agent. Monitor replication latency via sp_replmonitorsubscriptionpendingcmds or replication monitor. A lagging subscriber means data is stale.

sys.databases where mirroring_state IS NOT NULL (database mirroring configured). Database mirroring is deprecated since SQL Server 2012 and removed in SQL Server 2022. Migrate to Always On Availability Groups. Mirroring uses a single synchronous or asynchronous partner with no readable secondary and no multi-database consistency groups.

sys.databases where log_reuse_wait_desc = 'LOG_BACKUP' AND state_desc = 'RESTORING'. Log shipping is configured , this database is a warm standby. Monitor log shipping jobs in msdb.dbo.log_shipping_monitor_secondary. Check log_shipped_or_backed_up_date and restore_date , if the gap exceeds the recovery objective, the secondary is falling behind.

sys.database_permissions or sys.database_principals where elevated permissions (db_owner, CONTROL DATABASE, IMPERSONATE) are granted to accounts that should not require them. Elevated database permissions can be used for data exfiltration, privilege escalation, and bypassing row-level controls. Audit with: SELECT * FROM sys.database_principals dp JOIN sys.database_role_members drm ON dp.principal_id = drm.member_principal_id WHERE drm.role_principal_id = USER_ID('db_owner').

sys.databases where is_distributor = 1. This SQL instance is acting as a replication distributor. The distribution database holds pending replicated transactions. If the distributor falls behind, transactional replication latency increases. Monitor msdb..MSdistribution_agents and the distribution database size.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Availability Groups has automatically repaired corruption
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.dm_hadr_availability_replica_states where is_local = 0 and connected_state = 0 or synchronization_health < 2 , an AG replica triggered automatic page repair. While automatic page repair is a benefit of AG, repeated repairs indicate underlying storage hardware issues. Check Windows System event log for storage error events. Replace suspect hardware.

Reads msdb.dbo.suspect_pages (page_error_type > 0) or sys.databases where state_desc = 'SUSPECT'. A database in SUSPECT state is inaccessible. Causes: I/O subsystem failure, hardware fault, abrupt shutdown. Recovery path: restore from backup (preferred), or DBCC CHECKDB WITH REPAIR_ALLOW_DATA_LOSS (last resort , data loss accepted).

sys.databases where state_desc = 'SUSPECT'. A SUSPECT database cannot be accessed. This state results from transaction log or data file corruption during recovery after an unclean shutdown. Immediate action: restore from the most recent known-good backup. If no backup: ALTER DATABASE SET EMERGENCY, then DBCC CHECKDB WITH REPAIR_ALLOW_DATA_LOSS (data loss accepted).

sys.databases where state_desc = 'RECOVERY_PENDING'. SQL Server detected a resource issue during recovery startup. The database is not accessible. Causes: missing data or log file, insufficient disk space, or a log file that cannot be opened. Check SQL Server error log for the specific error during recovery.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Backing Up to Same Drive Where Databases Reside
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.master_files where physical_name LIKE '\%' (UNC path) or LIKE '\%' (network share). Database files on network shares (NFS, SMB) add network latency to every I/O operation and lose write-ahead durability guarantees if the share is disconnected. Local SAN-attached or DAS storage is strongly preferred. Network file storage is only acceptable with SMB 3.0 Multichannel or certified Azure Files configurations.

msdb.dbo.sysjobs , jobs with no failure notification operator configured. When a SQL Agent job fails silently, the DBA may not discover it until the impact is noticed (e.g. missing backup, stale index). Add an operator (msdb.dbo.sp_add_operator) and configure failure notifications on all critical jobs (backups, DBCC, index maintenance).

sys.dm_os_ring_buffers (SystemHealth) records per-minute CPU usage by SQL process. Sustained high CPU means queries are competing for compute cycles. Identify the top CPU consumers via sys.dm_exec_query_stats order by total_worker_time. Common causes: missing indexes, implicit conversions, poor cardinality estimates causing serial hash joins, or insufficient server hardware.

msdb.dbo.sysalerts , checks whether alert 832 (Corrupt page) or 833 (I/O taking > 15 seconds) is configured. No corruption alerts means data integrity failures go undetected until they surface as application errors. Configure alerts for severity 19â€“25 and error numbers 823, 824, 825 (I/O errors) and 832 (page corruption). Use Database Mail + operator to email the DBA team.

sys.dm_os_schedulers runnable_tasks_count and work_queue_count. A runnable_tasks_count consistently > 0 means threads are queued waiting for a scheduler slot (THREADPOOL waits). Increase max worker threads (sp_configure) or investigate why individual queries hold threads for extended durations.

sys.dm_os_sys_info cpu_count. Informational baseline. Compare against licensed core count; SQL Server Standard Edition caps at 24 cores or 4 sockets (whichever is lower). Verify CPU topology with sys.dm_os_nodes to ensure NUMA-aware scheduling is active.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Infinite merge replication metadata retention period
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats aggregate read_ios + write_ios per second. Sustained IOPS near the storage subsystem limit causes PAGEIOLATCH_SH/EX and WRITELOG waits. Identify the hottest files via io_stall_read_ms / num_of_reads and io_stall_write_ms / num_of_writes. Consider filegroup separation, SSD tiering, or read-scale AG replicas to offload read I/O.

Aggregates backup recency, DBCC CHECKDB last run date, and index fragmentation state. A failure here means the server is operating without fundamental DBA oversight. No backups + no integrity checks = undetected data corruption and no recovery path.

Cumulative MB read + written since last SQL restart, extrapolated to daily rate. High daily I/O volume is a leading indicator of future storage capacity issues. Compare against storage rated sequential throughput (MB/s). Also check for write amplification from excessive index fragmentation or log flush patterns.

sys.master_files , database files stored in Azure Blob Storage. Azure SQL Database Managed Instance or SQL Server data files in Azure Blob (FILESTREAM to Azure). Informational , confirm the Azure storage tier (Premium vs Standard) matches the I/O requirements and that redundancy (LRS/ZRS/GRS) meets the recovery objectives.

CPUID instruction or sys.dm_os_sys_info detects a hypervisor. Informational. Key considerations for virtualised SQL: NUMA alignment (ensure vNUMA matches pNUMA), memory balloon driver interference (disable it), vCPU over-commitment causing NUMA crossing, and snapshot-based VM backup conflicting with SQL VSS writer.

SERVERPROPERTY('ProcessID') checked against 64-bit indicators , or @@VERSION shows 'X86'. 32-bit SQL Server cannot access more than 4 GB of memory (with AWE enabled, up to ~3.5 GB without). Modern SQL workloads require 64-bit. This is almost certainly a legacy system that needs migration.

sys.objects where database_id = 1 (master) and type = 'X' (extended stored procedures) or user-created objects. Extended stored procedures in master run in-process with the SQL engine , a bug in an XP can crash the SQL Server process. Audit all non-system XPs in master: SELECT * FROM master.sys.objects WHERE type='X' AND is_ms_shipped=0.

sys.indexes where fill_factor != 0. Non-default fill factor affects page density , 70% fill factor leaves 30% empty on each index page for future inserts. On read-heavy tables with rare inserts, a low fill factor wastes buffer pool space. On insert-heavy tables, it prevents page splits. Validate fill factor choices against actual index usage stats from sys.dm_db_index_usage_stats.

sys.dm_os_wait_stats CXPACKET waits combined with cost threshold for parallelism = 5 (default). CXPACKET waits indicate parallel query execution with thread imbalance. The root fix is raising the cost threshold for parallelism (CTP) from 5 to 25â€“50. Also check MAXDOP: set to number of physical cores per NUMA node (not total logical processors). Monitor via sys.dm_os_waiting_tasks WHERE wait_type = 'CXPACKET'.

sys.dm_os_nodes WHERE node_state_desc = 'OFFLINE'. A NUMA node is offline, meaning some physical memory is inaccessible to SQL Server. This reduces available buffer pool below physical RAM. Causes: hardware failure, Windows NUMA topology misconfiguration, or BIOS NUMA settings. Check Windows System event log for memory module errors.

msdb.dbo.log_shipping_secondary or sys.databases where log_reuse_wait_desc = 'LOG_BACKUP' but no recent log backup exists. Log shipping secondary restore is failing or the backup job is not running. The secondary database is falling behind, increasing RPO exposure. Check SQL Agent jobs for log_shipping_backup_log and log_shipping_restore_log job statuses.

sys.databases where is_change_tracking_on = 1. Change Tracking records the primary keys of changed rows (not the data itself) in internal tables. Requires an application that actively consumes the change tracking data and calls CHANGE_TRACKING_MIN_VALID_VERSION. If not actively consumed, the internal cleanup may lag, bloating the change tracking tables. Verify the consumer application is running.

sys.fulltext_catalogs or sys.fulltext_indexes joined to sys.dm_fts_index_population WHERE status = 9 (failed). Full-text index population has failed or stalled. Application searches against these indexes return stale or no results. Check sys.dm_fts_index_population for error messages. Rebuild the catalog: ALTER FULLTEXT CATALOG [name] REBUILD.

sp_configure 'remote access' = 1. Remote access allows sp_executesql calls between SQL Server instances using the legacy remote server protocol (not linked servers). This is a deprecated feature removed in SQL Server 2022. Disable: sp_configure 'remote access', 0. Use linked servers or application-level data exchange instead.

sys.dm_os_wait_stats , CMEMTHREAD, SOS_WORK_DISPATCHER, or CXPACKET surges indicating parallelism 'rocket surgery' , excessive parallelism causing more overhead than benefit. Check: cost threshold for parallelism (should be > 25), MAXDOP (should not be 0 on NUMA systems), and Resource Governor parallelism limits. Review sys.dm_exec_requests WHERE parallel_workers_available = 0 for saturation evidence.

sp_configure 'backup compression default' = 0. Backup compression is available on SQL 2008 Standard and above. Without compression, backups are larger (60â€“80% larger typically), backup windows are longer, and storage costs are higher. Enable: sp_configure 'backup compression default', 1; RECONFIGURE.

sys.dm_os_wait_stats RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE and RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE_QUERY_COMPILE waits. These waits appear when queries cannot obtain their requested memory grant , they wait in a queue. Causes: max server memory too low, single query consuming excessive memory grant, or too many concurrent parallel queries. Tune max server memory to leave 10â€“15% for OS. Monitor sys.dm_exec_query_memory_grants for queued grants.

sys.databases joined to msdb.dbo.log_shipping_monitor_secondary. Log shipping is active. Monitor: (1) last_backup_date vs last_restored_date , the gap is the effective RPO, (2) SQL Agent jobs for backup, copy, and restore, (3) secondary server disk space for accumulating .trn files. An alert should fire when the restore lag exceeds the recovery objective.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: TDE Certificate Not Backed Up Recently
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.certificates WHERE expiry_date < GETDATE() + 30 (TDE certificate expiring soon) or no recent backup of the TDE certificate in the audit log. A TDE certificate that expires does NOT immediately break encryption but a future restore would require the certificate. The critical risk: if the certificate is lost (server failure, no backup), the encrypted database files are permanently inaccessible. EXEC sp_backup_database_encryption_key to back up the TDE certificate and its private key to a secure location.

Checks whether the SQL Server service account has the SE_MANAGE_VOLUME_NAME Windows privilege (Perform Volume Maintenance Tasks). Without IFI, every database file growth event must zero-fill the new space, causing long I/O stalls during autogrow. With IFI enabled, data file growth is instant. Enable via Windows Local Security Policy â†’ User Rights Assignment â†’ Perform volume maintenance tasks â†’ add the SQL Server service account. Does NOT apply to log files (always zero-filled for write-ahead logging safety).

sys.stats where auto_created = 0 AND user_created = 1 (non-index statistics created manually). User-created statistics are updated at the same threshold as auto-created stats but may conflict with index statistics. They add overhead to statistics update jobs. Review whether manual statistics are still needed or whether an index covers the same columns.

Reads Windows power scheme via registry or WMI. Balanced/Power Saver plans use P-states (frequency scaling) and C-states (CPU sleep), capping SQL query throughput and adding microsecond latency to CPU wake-up cycles. Set the High Performance plan: powercfg /setactive 8c5e7fda-e8bf-4a96-9a85-a6e23a8c635c. Also check the hypervisor power policy on virtual machines , the host must also use High Performance.

Reads system_health Extended Events session for xml_deadlock_report events within the last 24 hours. Deadlocks indicate two or more sessions hold locks the other needs. Investigation: run sys.dm_exec_query_stats to find the queries involved; review the deadlock graph XML in sys.fn_xe_file_target_read_file for the system_health session. Mitigation: consistent index access order, shorter transactions, appropriate isolation level, or RCSI.

sys.dm_os_plan_cache_stats or DBCC FREEPROCCACHE events in the default trace. The plan cache was cleared recently (DBCC FREEPROCCACHE or ALTER DATABASE SCOPED CONFIGURATION CLEAR PROCEDURE_CACHE). Clearing the plan cache causes a CPU spike as all statements recompile simultaneously on next execution , a form of self-inflicted 'recompile storm'. Identify who ran this via the default trace.

sp_configure 'priority boost' = 1. Priority boost raises the SQL Server process Windows scheduling priority from Normal to High. This can starve other OS processes (including the Network stack) of CPU, causing the OS to become unresponsive. Microsoft explicitly recommends against enabling priority boost. Disable: sp_configure 'priority boost', 0; RECONFIGURE.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: SQL Server Version Out of Support
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: Compares @@VERSION build number (SELECT SERVERPROPERTY('ProductVersion')) against the Microsoft end-of-support dates table embedded in sp_Blitz. An out-of-support SQL version receives no security patches, no bug fixes, and no support from Microsoft. Impact: compliance breach, unpatched CVEs, and increased risk of data corruption from unfixed bugs. Plan an in-place upgrade or migration to a supported version.

sp_configure 'number of error log files' , default is 6. With SQL cycling error logs on restart, 6 files may only retain a few days of history on busy instances. Increase to 26 or more: EXEC xp_instance_regwrite N'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE', N'Software\Microsoft\MSSQLServer\MSSQLServer', N'NumErrorLogs', REG_DWORD, 26. Retain at least 30 days of error log history.

sys.objects joined to sys.extended_properties WHERE value = 'startup'. Stored procedures marked as startup execute automatically on every SQL Server instance start with sysadmin privileges before any logins. Review each startup proc: verify its purpose is documented, it cannot be hijacked by a non-sysadmin, and it is still required. A rogue startup proc is an effective persistence mechanism for an attacker who has gained sysadmin.

sys.databases is_supplemental_logging_enabled = 1. Supplemental logging adds additional context to the transaction log for replication/CDC consumers. Increases log volume. Confirm it is required by an active log consumer; disable if not.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Non-Default DB Option: Snapshot Isolation Enabled
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.databases snapshot_isolation_state_desc = 'ON'. SNAPSHOT isolation reads row versions from tempdb rather than acquiring shared locks. Reduces read/write blocking but increases tempdb I/O and version store size. Monitor tempdb version store growth via sys.dm_tran_version_store_space_usage.

sys.databases is_read_committed_snapshot_on = 1. RCSI changes the default READ COMMITTED isolation level to use row versions. Dramatically reduces lock contention in OLTP workloads. Requires sufficient tempdb capacity. RCSI + SNAPSHOT can coexist; RCSI is recommended for most OLTP systems if tempdb can handle the version store.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Non-Default DB Option: Auto Create Stats Incremental
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.databases is_auto_create_stats_incremental_on = 1. Incremental statistics update per-partition rather than the full table. Reduces statistics update time on very large partitioned tables but requires partition-aware applications to benefit. SQL 2014+.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Non-Default DB Option: ANSI NULL Default
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.databases is_ansi_null_default_on = 1. Changes default column nullability from NOT NULL to NULL in CREATE TABLE. Can silently change schema intent. Applications that rely on explicit NOT NULL declarations should not be affected; but auto-generated schema tools may produce unexpected results.

sys.databases is_recursive_triggers_on = 1. Allows a trigger to fire triggers of the same type recursively. Without a recursion guard (@@NESTLEVEL check or recursion counter), recursive triggers can cause infinite loops and transaction log exhaustion. Audit all triggers in databases where this is enabled.

Same as check 75. TRUSTWORTHY = ON is a privilege escalation vector. Any db_owner can create a SAFE assembly or use EXECUTE AS to access sa-level objects. Disable it; use database-scoped credentials and certificates for cross-database access instead.

sys.databases is_parameterization_forced = 1. Forces all ad-hoc queries to be parameterized (like SIMPLE parameterization but more aggressive). Reduces plan cache bloat but can cause poor plan choices when plans are reused across parameters with very different selectivity. Use sp_executesql for application queries instead.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Non-Default DB Option: Query Store Enabled
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.databases is_query_store_on = 1. Informational. Query Store should be enabled on all SQL 2016+ databases. Check Query Store configuration: MAX_STORAGE_SIZE_MB, QUERY_CAPTURE_MODE, CLEANUP_POLICY. A full Query Store silently stops collecting , monitor sys.database_query_store_options.current_storage_size_mb vs max_storage_size_mb.

sys.databases is_cdc_enabled = 1. Change Data Capture requires SQL Agent and retains log records until the CDC cleanup job runs. Verify CDC capture and cleanup jobs are running in SQL Agent. Monitor log file growth , if cleanup is not running, the log will grow indefinitely. Check sys.dm_cdc_log_scan_sessions for scan latency.

sys.databases where containment != 0. Contained databases allow users to authenticate at the database level without instance-level logins. Reduces centralised identity management visibility. Ensure CHECK_POLICY = ON for contained users and monitor authentication failures in the SQL error log.

sys.databases where target_recovery_time_seconds != 0. Non-zero target recovery time changes the database recovery interval, affecting checkpoint frequency. Longer recovery time = less frequent checkpoints = longer recovery after crash. The default 0 uses the server-level 'recovery interval' sp_configure setting. Non-zero values are used to extend recovery time for write-heavy OLTP databases to reduce checkpoint I/O.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Delayed Durability Enabled, Accepted Data Loss Risk
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.databases where delayed_durability_desc != 'DISABLED'. Delayed durability allows a COMMIT to return before the log record is hardened to disk. On an uncontrolled shutdown, up to one log block (~60 KB) of committed transactions can be lost. Only acceptable for workloads that explicitly tolerate data loss (e.g. high-frequency telemetry inserts).

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Non-Default DB Option: In-Memory OLTP Enabled
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: Presence of MEMORY_OPTIMIZED_DATA filegroup or sys.databases memory_optimized_elevate_to_snapshot = 1. In-Memory OLTP (Hekaton) provides order-of-magnitude throughput for suitable OLTP workloads. Not all T-SQL features are supported in natively compiled procedures. Monitor memory-optimised table memory via sys.dm_db_xtp_memory_consumers.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: High Memory Use for In-Memory OLTP (Hekaton)
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.dm_os_memory_clerks WHERE type = 'MEMORYCLERK_XTP' , In-Memory OLTP memory clerk consuming excessive RAM. Memory-optimised tables hold all data in RAM permanently (no eviction to disk). If a memory-optimised table grows beyond available memory, SQL Server cannot accept inserts. Monitor memory usage: SELECT * FROM sys.dm_db_xtp_memory_consumers ORDER BY allocated_bytes DESC.

sys.tables where is_memory_optimized = 1. In-Memory OLTP (Hekaton) tables are present. Informational , confirms the feature is in use. Key management items: (1) MEMORY_OPTIMIZED_DATA filegroup must be on reliable fast storage, (2) checkpoint pairs accumulate until the merge process runs, (3) natively compiled procedures have limited T-SQL surface area.

sys.dm_db_xtp_transactions WHERE transaction_id > 0 AND result = 'ABORTED_DUE_TO_CONFLICT'. In-Memory OLTP optimistic concurrency causes transaction aborts when write-write conflicts occur. High abort rates indicate two sessions frequently modifying the same rows. Review the application's transaction patterns , use SNAPSHOT isolation consistently in the application layer.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Database Files on Network File Shares
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.master_files WHERE physical_name LIKE '\%' (UNC network paths). Database files on network file shares introduce network latency into every SQL I/O operation. If the network path is unavailable, the database goes offline. Only certified file share configurations (SMB 3.0 with WSFC-aware NAS) are supported. Use direct-attached or SAN storage whenever possible.

sys.master_files WHERE physical_name LIKE 'https:%'. Database files stored in Azure Blob Storage (remote storage). SQL Server 2012+ supports storing .mdf/.ldf in Azure Blob for specific cloud scenarios. Latency is higher than local SSD. Ensure the Azure Premium storage tier is used for data and log files. Monitor io_stall via sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Errors Logged Recently in the Default Trace
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: Reads sys.dm_os_ring_buffers WHERE ring_buffer_type = 'RING_BUFFER_EXCEPTION' and the SQL Server error log via xp_readerrorlog for severity >= 17 errors. Recent high-severity errors (errors 17â€“25) indicate resource exhaustion or internal failures. Investigate via SELECT * FROM sys.dm_os_ring_buffers WHERE ring_buffer_type = 'RING_BUFFER_EXCEPTION'.

sys.dm_io_pending_io_requests or sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats , autogrow events taking more than the expected duration. Slow autogrow events block the session that triggered them (data files without IFI, all log files). Prevent autogrow in production: pre-size files and set autogrow only as a safety net with a fixed large increment. Monitor with sys.dm_io_pending_io_requests during off-hours expansion.

sys.database_scoped_configurations or sys.databases for non-default option values. A catch-all check for database options that deviate from defaults. Review the specific option flagged , most non-default values are either intentional tuning decisions or legacy settings left over from an earlier DBA. Document all non-default options in a configuration baseline.

sys.dm_os_wait_stats shows no single dominant wait type above the significance threshold. Informational , the server appears to have headroom. Note: this check fires when sp_Blitz does NOT find a problem, meaning it is a positive health indicator. Could also indicate wait stats were recently cleared.

@@VERSION contains 'X86' or SERVERPROPERTY('ProcessID') resolves to a 32-bit process. 32-bit SQL Server is a legacy concern. Maximum addressable memory is 4 GB (2 GB user space without /3GB switch). All modern SQL Server versions are 64-bit. This system needs immediate migration.

sp_Blitz reports its own version. This informational check flags when the installed First Responder Kit (sp_Blitz et al.) is outdated. Newer versions add checks and fixes for recent SQL Server builds; update the kit periodically so the assessment stays current. No production impact — assessment-tooling hygiene only.
Rundate marker check , sp_Blitz records its own run timestamp. Used internally for delta calculations. Informational.

@@VERSION compared against a list of builds with known critical security CVEs. A build with a critical security vulnerability is flagged. Check Microsoft Security Bulletins for the specific CVE numbers. Apply the relevant security patch (GDR release) or full CU immediately.

sys.master_files where growth = 1024 KB (1 MB autogrow). 1 MB autogrow creates a new VLF for every megabyte of log growth, resulting in thousands of VLFs over time (DBCC LOGINFO). High VLF count causes slow database startup recovery and log backup overhead. Correct: ALTER DATABASE [db] MODIFY FILE (NAME='...', FILEGROWTH=512MB) for log files. Then cycle log to reclaim VLFs.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Memory Dangerously Low in NUMA Nodes
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.dm_os_nodes WHERE memory_node_id > 0 and online_scheduler_count = 0 or memory_utilization_percentage > 95. A NUMA node has critically low available memory. When one NUMA node is memory-starved, local allocation fails and remote NUMA allocation occurs , increasing memory access latency significantly. Investigate memory pressure on the specific NUMA node via sys.dm_os_memory_nodes.

sys.dm_exec_cached_plans , counts query_hash values with many distinct plan_handle entries. Multiple plans for the same query indicate parameter sensitivity or implicit type conversion preventing plan reuse. Use sys.dm_exec_query_plan to compare plans and identify the differing elements. Candidates for OPTION(OPTIMIZE FOR) or Query Store plan forcing.

sys.dm_exec_cached_plans WHERE usecounts > 1 , high plan count indicates cache pressure or excessive parameterisation variety. Check plan cache via: SELECT TOP 10 usecounts, size_in_bytes, cacheobjtype, objtype, sys.dm_exec_sql_text(plan_handle) FROM sys.dm_exec_cached_plans CROSS APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(plan_handle) ORDER BY usecounts DESC.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Poison Wait Detected: CMEMTHREAD and NUMA
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.database_scoped_configurations QUERY_OPTIMIZER_HOTFIXES = ON at database level. Enables post-RTM query optimizer fixes (equivalent to trace flag 4199) for this specific database. This is a targeted enablement , the fix applies only to this database. Verify the specific optimizer fix needed and confirm it resolves the original problem without regressions.

sys.databases where is_query_store_on = 0. Query Store is not enabled. Query Store (SQL 2016+) captures query plans and runtime statistics automatically. Without it: no plan regression detection, no plan forcing, no historical performance baseline. Enable: ALTER DATABASE [db] SET QUERY_STORE = ON (READ_WRITE) WITH (MAX_STORAGE_SIZE_MB = 2048, QUERY_CAPTURE_MODE = AUTO).

sys.plan_guides WHERE is_disabled = 0 , a plan guide exists but is failing (plan guide match fails and query runs without the guide). When a plan guide fails silently, the intended query plan is not applied. Check: SELECT * FROM sys.plan_guides WHERE is_disabled = 0. Test: use SET SHOWPLAN_ALL ON and verify the guide is being used. Repair or drop broken plan guides.

sys.dm_os_process_memory , process working set is significantly lower than committed memory, indicating memory pressure is forcing pages to disk (paging). SQL Server buffer pool is being paged out. Add RAM or reduce max server memory to leave more headroom for the OS.

sys.availability_groups and sys.dm_hadr_availability_replica_states. An Always On Availability Group is configured. Key health checks: primary/secondary roles, synchronisation state (SYNCHRONIZED vs SYNCHRONIZING vs NOT SYNCHRONIZING), and log send queue / redo queue sizes in sys.dm_hadr_database_replica_states. Alert on log_send_queue_size > recovery_point_objective threshold.

SQL Server Agent service status via sys.dm_server_services WHERE servicename LIKE '%Agent%' and status_desc != 'Running'. The SQL Agent is offline. All scheduled jobs (backups, maintenance, alerting) are not running. High-priority fix , restart the Agent service and investigate why it stopped.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Full-text Filter Daemon Launcher is Currently Offline
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.dm_fts_active_catalogs or sys.dm_server_services WHERE servicename LIKE '%Full-text%'. The Full-Text Filter Daemon Launcher service (fdlaunch.exe) is stopped or offline. Without it, full-text index population pauses and full-text queries against indexes that need updating return stale results. Start the service: net start MSSQLFDLauncher (or MSSQLFDLauncher$INSTANCENAME for named instances). Configure it to start automatically.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: SQL Server is running under an NT Service account
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.columns where encryption_type IS NOT NULL. Column-level encryption (CLE via EncryptByKey/EncryptByCert, or Always Encrypted via column master key). Informational , confirms encryption is in place for sensitive data. Verify key rotation is scheduled and backup of encryption keys/certificates is current (separate from database backup).

blitzCheckMap.findingName: SQL Server Agent is running under an NT Service account
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.dm_server_services WHERE servicename LIKE '%Agent%' and service_account LIKE 'NT Service%'. SQL Agent is running as the built-in NT Service account. This account has limited network access , it cannot write to network shares, access Active Directory, or perform remote operations required by some jobs (log shipping, SSIS, linked server jobs). Configure a dedicated domain service account with minimum required permissions.

sys.dm_server_services WHERE dump_path IS NOT NULL , recent memory dump (.mdmp) files detected. SQL Server generates a minidump or full dump on access violations, scheduling violations (scheduler stuck), or out-of-memory conditions. Each dump represents an internal SQL Server error. Open the dump in WinDbg or send to Microsoft PSS. Review the SQL Server error log for the corresponding error message.

sys.databases where is_accelerated_database_recovery_on = 1. Accelerated Database Recovery (ADR) , SQL 2019+ , dramatically reduces recovery time after crash and long rollback operations by maintaining a persistent version store in the database. Increases database file growth (version store). Monitor ADR cleanup and version store size via sys.dm_tran_persistent_version_store_stats.

SERVERPROPERTY('Edition') contains 'Developer', 'Express', or 'Evaluation'. A non-production licence running a production workload violates Microsoft licensing terms and may have capacity limits (Express: 10 GB database size cap, 1 socket / 4 cores). Evaluate whether the correct production licence is in place.

sp_configure 'buffer pool extension enabled' = 1 (SQL 2014â€“2019 only). Buffer Pool Extension (BPE) uses an SSD as an extension of the buffer pool for caching read-only pages. Deprecated and removed in SQL 2022. Performance benefit is marginal , adding more RAM is almost always better. Monitor via sys.dm_os_buffer_pool_extension_configuration.

sys.master_files WHERE database_id = 2 (tempdb) and file_type = 0 (data files) , count exceeds 16. Having more than 8â€“16 tempdb data files provides no additional benefit and complicates management. SQL's proportional fill algorithm becomes less effective with very large file counts. The recommended maximum is 8 files (or equal to logical CPU count if fewer than 8). Extra files beyond the recommended count do not reduce contention and may increase management overhead.

sys.dm_xe_sessions where name LIKE '%' and event count exceeds a threshold. Extended Events sessions are writing too many events , likely capturing high-frequency events without adequate filtering (e.g. sql_statement_completed without WHERE filter). Review session definitions: SELECT xes.name, xe.event_name FROM sys.dm_xe_sessions xes JOIN sys.dm_xe_session_events xe ON xes.address = xe.event_session_address.

sys.configurations WHERE name = 'default trace enabled' = 0 or related internal monitoring feature disabled. Default trace, DMV retention, or other built-in diagnostic features have been disabled. These provide zero-cost forensic data that is invaluable for incident investigation. Re-enable unless replaced with equivalent coverage.

sys.databases where source_database_id IS NOT NULL (database snapshot). Database snapshots hold a sparse file copy of the original database at the snapshot creation point. They grow as pages change in the source database, consuming disk space. Old snapshots cause source database disk writes to double (CoW mechanism). List and drop stale snapshots: SELECT * FROM sys.databases WHERE source_database_id IS NOT NULL.

sys.dm_os_loaded_modules WHERE name NOT LIKE '%Microsoft%' and NOT LIKE '%Windows%'. Third-party DLLs loaded in the SQL Server address space (antivirus, backup agents, monitoring tools) can cause access violations, memory corruption, or scheduler non-yielding conditions. Review each module. Configure antivirus to exclude SQL Server process memory scanning.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Shrink Database Step In Maintenance Plan
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.dm_exec_query_plan , detects maintenance plan steps with DBCC SHRINKDATABASE or SHRINKFILE. A shrink step in a maintenance plan re-fragments every index immediately after the rebuild step. Remove shrink steps from all maintenance plans. If free space is a concern, reclaim it by archiving data or adding storage.

msdb.dbo.sysmaintplan_subplans with repetitive steps. SQL Agent maintenance plan XML contains duplicate or overlapping steps (e.g. multiple REBUILD INDEX steps). Maintenance plans are inflexible compared to Ola Hallengren's maintenance solution. Consolidate to a single well-sequenced plan or migrate to IndexOptimize/DatabaseBackup scripts.

sys.databases where is_query_store_on = 1 and query_store_state = 'READ_ONLY' because max_storage_size is reached. Query Store stops capturing new data when the size limit is reached. Check: SELECT * FROM sys.database_query_store_options. Fix: increase MAX_STORAGE_SIZE_MB or set CLEANUP_POLICY (STALE_QUERY_THRESHOLD_DAYS). Run: ALTER DATABASE [db] SET QUERY_STORE CLEAR to flush old data if necessary.

sys.master_files for tempdb , data files are not equal in size. SQL uses proportional fill to distribute writes across tempdb files equally. If files are unequal, the larger file gets proportionally more I/O. Pre-size all tempdb data files equally at startup. After adding files or resizing, restart SQL to reinitialize.

sys.availability_groups where there are no remaining failover-ready replicas (all secondaries are either DISCONNECTED or SYNCHRONIZING). If the primary fails now, manual failover is required (no automatic failover available). Investigate secondary replica connectivity: SELECT replica_server_name, operational_state_desc, connected_state_desc FROM sys.dm_hadr_availability_replica_states.

Detects that DBCC SQLPERF('sys.dm_os_wait_stats', CLEAR) was recently executed. This resets all cumulative wait counters, invalidating any trend analysis. Identify who ran this and document why. Clearing wait stats should be a deliberate, documented decision , not a routine action.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: MSDB Backup History Purged Too Frequently
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: msdb.dbo.backupset WHERE backup_finish_date > DATEADD(DAY, -7, GETDATE()) , backup history for a database was purged within the past week. Excessive backup history purges destroy the audit trail needed to determine the last known-good restore point. Set a longer retention policy for msdb backup history: EXEC msdb.dbo.sp_delete_backuphistory with a date further in the past.

sys.endpoints where principal_id != sa and the endpoint owner is an unprivileged login. Endpoint ownership allows the owner to change endpoint configuration, potentially enabling unauthenticated connections. Reassign endpoint ownership to sa: ALTER AUTHORIZATION ON ENDPOINT::[name] TO sa.

sp_configure 'cost threshold for parallelism' = 5 (default low value). A CTP of 5 means virtually any non-trivial query is eligible for parallelism. On OLTP servers, this causes excessive CXPACKET waits and serial bottlenecks at the thread synchronisation point. Recommended: raise CTP to 25â€“50 for mixed OLTP/OLAP; higher (50â€“100) for pure OLTP. Test changes during off-peak with Query Store plan regression monitoring.

sp_configure 'min server memory (MB)' = 0 AND 'max server memory (MB)' = default. Dynamic memory mode means SQL can both request and release memory freely. On dedicated SQL servers this is acceptable; on shared servers where other processes compete for RAM, set explicit min/max to prevent buffer pool eviction.

Compares sys.master_files (tempdb file count at startup) against tempdb.sys.database_files (current file count). A mismatch means tempdb files were added after startup without restarting SQL, so the new files are empty until SQL restarts. Ensure tempdb data file count = logical CPU count (up to 8). Restart SQL after adding tempdb files to equalize fill.

sys.databases where compatibility_level < the instance version compatibility level. Lower compatibility disables newer optimizer features (IQP features, CE improvements, etc.). Test upgrading compatibility level in a lower environment. Use Query Store to capture regression baselines before and after the change. Target: compatibility level = current SQL Server version.

sys.databases where compatibility_level < SERVERPROPERTY('ProductMajorVersion') * 10 + 90 (or similar). Specific compatibility level check for feature gaps. Databases below compat 130 (SQL 2016) cannot use Query Store, RCSI improvements, or IQP. Below 140 (SQL 2017): no adaptive joins or batch mode memory grant feedback. Below 150 (SQL 2019): no row mode MGF, scalar UDF inlining, or table variable deferred compilation.

sys.database_scoped_configurations where name = 'MAXDOP'. A per-database MAXDOP overrides the instance setting for all queries in that database. Confirm it was set intentionally. Inconsistent MAXDOP across databases complicates performance troubleshooting and can cause unexpected serialisation or excessive parallelism depending on the value.

sys.database_scoped_configurations LEGACY_CARDINALITY_ESTIMATION = ON. Forces the SQL Server 2012 CE model regardless of compatibility level. Typically a workaround applied after a CE upgrade caused a specific query regression. Should be reviewed , the modern CE generally produces better plans for most workloads. Identify the offending query and apply OPTION(USE HINT('FORCE_LEGACY_CARDINALITY_ESTIMATION')) at query level instead.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Parameter Sniffing Disabled at Database Level
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.database_scoped_configurations PARAMETER_SNIFFING = OFF. Disables parameter sniffing globally , SQL uses average statistics instead of the sniffed value. This is a blunt workaround for parameter-sensitive plan problems. It improves some queries at the expense of others. Prefer OPTION(OPTIMIZE FOR UNKNOWN), OPTION(RECOMPILE), or Query Store plan forcing for targeted fixes.

sys.database_scoped_configurations QUERY_OPTIMIZER_HOTFIXES = ON. Enables optimizer bug-fixes shipped post-RTM that are otherwise off by default for backward compatibility (equivalent to trace flag 4199). Generally recommended to enable , optimizer fixes address known cardinality and plan quality bugs with low regression risk.

sys.dm_os_sys_info WHERE cpu_count is odd (not a power of 2). An odd CPU count typically indicates misconfigured VM vCPU allocation or a CPU affinity mask cutting off half of a core pair. Odd CPU counts can cause suboptimal NUMA alignment and MAXDOP configuration. Correct the VM vCPU allocation to an even number aligned to the physical core topology.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: There Is An Error With The Default Trace
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.traces WHERE is_default = 1 , the default trace exists but may have errors or be writing to an unusual location. Check that the default trace file is accessible and not filling a restricted drive. Verify via SELECT * FROM sys.traces WHERE is_default = 1.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Encryption Certificate Not Backed Up Recently
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.certificates WHERE thumbprint = [TDE cert thumbprint] and last_backup_date < threshold. Encryption certificates used for Always Encrypted, TDE, or Service Broker have not been backed up recently. Certificate loss = permanent loss of encrypted data. Back up all encryption certificates to a secure, separate location on a scheduled basis.

Reads default trace (fn_trace_gettable) for DBCC events (EventClass 116). Filters out CHECKDB and CHECKTABLE. High-impact DBCC commands (SHRINKFILE, SHRINKDATABASE, DBREINDEX, CHECKALLOC) run by users indicate unplanned maintenance or attempts to recover a problem. Investigate who ran the command and why.

default trace or sys.dm_os_wait_stats reset timestamp , auto-shrink ran recently (DBCC SHRINKDATABASE or SHRINKFILE event in trace). Shrink operations devastate index fragmentation and cause I/O storms. Investigate why auto-shrink ran and disable it immediately.

default trace , DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS event detected. This flushes all clean pages from the buffer pool, forcing every subsequent data access to read from storage. Used in benchmarks to simulate cold cache , never appropriate in production. Identify who ran this: SELECT * FROM sys.fn_trace_gettable(DEFAULT, DEFAULT) WHERE EventClass = 116 AND TextData LIKE '%DROPCLEANBUFFERS%'.

default trace , DBCC FREEPROCCACHE event. All cached execution plans were flushed. The next execution of every stored procedure and ad-hoc statement requires full recompilation. Causes a CPU spike and elevated response times until the cache warms up. Identify and investigate , this should never be run on a production system without change management approval.

default trace , DBCC WRITEPAGE event. This command directly modifies a database page in the buffer pool, bypassing all normal transaction mechanisms. It is used by Microsoft internal debugging only and can cause irreversible data corruption if misused. This event in production is a critical incident , identify who ran it immediately and assess data integrity.

sys.dm_exec_query_stats , OPTION(FORCE ORDER) or OPTION(LOOP JOIN) hints detected in query text via dm_exec_sql_text. Queries with optimizer hint instructions constrain the optimizer's ability to adapt to data changes. These hints are usually workarounds for specific historical plan problems. Review whether the underlying issue (statistics staleness, bad cardinality estimate) can be addressed instead.

Reads sys.dm_exec_query_stats or default trace for DBCC SHRINKDATABASE / SHRINKFILE events. SHRINK operations move data pages toward the front of the file, causing massive random I/O and fragmentation of all indexes in the database. After shrink, every index is fragmented. Never shrink databases as a space reclamation strategy , reclaim space by deleting or archiving data instead.

Reads backup retention settings , checks whether a backup retention policy is configured that retains sufficient history. Insufficient backup retention means point-in-time recovery beyond the retention window is impossible. Ensure backup retention meets the RPO defined in the SLA. For FULL+DIFF+LOG strategies, retain at minimum: 2 weeks FULL, 2 days DIFF, and LOG backups for the entire window.

sys.dm_exec_requests or sys.dm_os_waiting_tasks WHERE wait_type LIKE 'PAGELATCH%' targeting tempdb allocation bitmaps (pages 1:1, 1:2, 1:3). Tempdb PFS/GAM/SGAM contention occurs when too few tempdb data files force all allocation bitmap updates through a single set of pages. Resolve by adding tempdb data files (target: 1 per logical CPU, max 8). SQL 2016+ trace flag 1118 behaviour is default.

sys.databases where owner_sid is a binary SID that cannot be resolved to any current server principal. The database owner login has been dropped from the instance. A dropped database owner causes issues with: sp_changedbowner, cross-database ownership chain resolution, and some backup/restore operations. Reassign: ALTER AUTHORIZATION ON DATABASE::[db] TO [sa].

sys.master_files where database_id = 2 (tempdb). Checks whether tempdb file count, size, and location are optimised. Best practice: (1) one data file per logical CPU (max 8), (2) equal size for all files, (3) separate drive from system/user databases, (4) autogrow disabled (pre-size instead). Misconfigurations cause allocation contention and unexpected I/O spikes.

sys.dm_exec_sessions or sys.dm_tran_active_transactions with open_transaction_count > 0 and transaction type indicating implicit transaction mode. Implicit transactions (SET IMPLICIT_TRANSACTIONS ON) auto-begin a transaction on certain statements and require explicit COMMIT. Forgotten COMMITs cause long-running open transactions, escalating lock holdings and blocking other sessions. Check via DBCC OPENTRAN.

sys.dm_exec_requests where sql_handle references a paused resumable index operation. A resumable index rebuild/create operation was started and paused. It is holding space in tempdb and consuming maintenance window resources. Resume with ALTER INDEX [name] ON [table] RESUME or abort with ALTER INDEX [name] ON [table] ABORT.

Compares @@VERSION against the latest known CU for the installed release. A newer CU is available. CUs contain cumulative bug fixes and may include security patches. Test in a lower environment first. Establish a regular patching cadence.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Objects created with dangerous SET Options
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.sql_modules where uses_ansi_nulls = 0 or uses_quoted_identifier = 0. Objects compiled with non-standard SET options fail when used with filtered indexes, indexed views, and CLR objects. Recreate affected objects with correct SET options. Run: SELECT name, uses_ansi_nulls, uses_quoted_identifier FROM sys.sql_modules sm JOIN sys.objects o ON sm.object_id=o.object_id WHERE uses_ansi_nulls=0 OR uses_quoted_identifier=0.

msdb.dbo.sysalerts where message_id IS NOT NULL and event_description_keyword IS NULL. An alert is configured to fire on a specific error message number but has no description keyword filter. The alert fires on any occurrence of that error number , including expected, handled errors. Add a keyword filter to reduce noise or remove alerts that fire too frequently to be actionable.

sys.dm_xe_sessions , an Extended Events session beyond the default system_health session is running. Informational , XE sessions capture diagnostic data but add overhead proportional to event frequency. Review active sessions via SELECT name, total_regular_buffers, total_large_buffers FROM sys.dm_xe_sessions. Ensure sessions are not running EVENT_RETENTION_MODE = NO_EVENT_LOSS (can block SQL threads).

sys.dm_os_sys_info WHERE sqlserver_start_time > DATEADD(HOUR, -24, GETDATE()). SQL Server restarted within the past 24 hours. A recent restart means: (1) the plan cache is empty (all queries recompiling), (2) buffer pool is cold (all reads from storage), (3) wait statistics are only 24 hours old. Performance will be atypically poor until the cache warms. Investigate why the restart occurred if unplanned.

sys.traces WHERE is_default = 0 , a non-default (user-created) SQL trace is running. SQL Trace is deprecated. Active traces consume CPU and I/O for every traced event. Migrate non-default traces to Extended Events sessions. The default trace (trace_id 1) is expected and useful; other active traces should be reviewed for necessity.

sys.availability_replicas , secondary replica configuration. Checks whether readable secondaries, backup preference, or automatic failover settings are configured appropriately. A misconfigured replica (e.g. AUTO_FAILOVER with SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT on a WAN link) risks unexpected failovers on network jitter.

sys.query_store_plan WHERE is_forced_plan = 1. Query Store plan forcing is active. Forced plans are a deliberate workaround for plan regressions. Monitor via sys.query_store_plan , forced plans may become invalid if the underlying schema changes (index drop, statistics update). Set up alerts for force_failure_count > 0 in sys.query_store_plan.

sys.dm_exec_requests or sys.indexes , a resumable index operation is paused. It holds locks on the table object (Sch-M at start/end) and consumes space in tempdb. Resume or abort to release resources.

The First Responder Kit scripts (sp_Blitz, sp_BlitzCache, sp_BlitzFirst, etc.) are community-maintained SQL Server health assessment tools. This informational check identifies that the kit is installed and provides version metadata. Keep the kit updated to receive latest checks and fixes for your SQL Server version.
SERVERPROPERTY('Edition') = 'Evaluation Edition'.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Non-Default DB Option: Service Broker Enabled
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.databases is_broker_enabled = 1. Service Broker provides asynchronous, reliable message queuing within SQL Server. If not actively used, an enabled broker adds background activity. If used, monitor sys.transmission_queue for poison messages and ensure conversation lifetime management (END CONVERSATION) is implemented. Poison messages can stop a queue entirely.

blitzCheckMap.findingName: Non-Default DB Option: Service Broker Priority
blitzCheckMap.business_translation: sys.databases is_honor_broker_priority_on = 1. Enables conversation priorities for Service Broker. Only relevant if Service Broker is actively used with prioritised message types.

232 / OPTIMIZE_FOR_AD_HOC_WORKLOADS scoped config. Stores only a plan stub on first execution rather than a full compiled plan. Reduces plan cache bloat from single-use ad hoc queries. Equivalent to sp_configure 'optimize for ad hoc workloads'. Positive change , should be enabled on OLTP systems with variable ad hoc workloads.

sys.database_scoped_configurations XTP_PROCEDURE_EXECUTION_STATISTICS = ON. Collects module-level execution statistics for natively compiled T-SQL. Adds overhead proportional to natively compiled procedure call rate. Enable temporarily for In-Memory OLTP performance investigation; disable in production.

sys.database_scoped_configurations XTP_QUERY_EXECUTION_STATISTICS = ON. Statement-level statistics for natively compiled modules. Higher overhead than module-level. Use only during active In-Memory OLTP investigations.
